pr.title: Persist API keys in SQLite
pr.body:
## Summary
- Added an `ApiKey` SQLAlchemy model plus helper functions to save and load provider keys from SQLite.
- Replaced the hard-coded IEX sandbox token with database lookup and `IEX_API_KEY` environment fallback.
- Added `manage_api_keys.py` CLI and README instructions for saving/listing API keys so they persist across server restarts.

## Review & Testing Checklist for Human
- [ ] Run `python manage_api_keys.py set iex YOUR_IEX_API_KEY`, restart the server, and verify stock info still loads.
- [ ] Confirm `python manage_api_keys.py list` shows `iex` without printing the secret value.
- [ ] Verify the stock info page redirects with an error message if no IEX API key exists.

### Notes
Validated with Python 3.9 dependency install, `py_compile`, and a smoke test that stores/reads/removes a temporary API key in SQLite. Python 3.12 dependency install is not compatible with the pinned `greenlet==1.1.2`, so Python 3.9 was used.

Link to Devin session: https://app.devin.ai/sessions/e7545c5491764fc3818a977f4a314ac9
Requested by: @zawceo
pr.author: devin-ai-integration[bot]
